SSLError
    If the eof was unexpected. The raised ssl error has `errno` set as `SSL_ERROR_EOF`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_pipe.SSLPipe.feed_ssl_data
documentation:
Feed SSL record level data into the pipe.

# Parameters

data

: If empty `bytes` is given, then it will get ssl_data for the handshake initialization.

# Returns

ssl_data

: Contains the SSL data that needs to be sent to the remote SSL.

application_data

: Plaintext data that needs to be forwarded to the application.

  Might contain an empty `bytes`, what indicates that `.shutdown` should be called.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_pipe.SSLPipe.feed_application_data
documentation:
Feed plaintext data into the pipe.

If the returned `offset` is less than the returned data's total length in bytes, that case is called
`short write`. If `short-write` happens, than the same `data` should be passed to `.feed_application_data`
next time. (This is an OpenSSL requirement.) A further particularity is that a short write will always have
`offset = 0`, because the `_ssl` module does not enable partial writes. And even though the offset is `0`,
there will still be encrypted data in ssl_data.

# Returns

ssl_data

: Containing record level data that needs to be sent to the remote SSL instance.

offset

: The number of the processed plaintext data in bytes. Might be less than the total length of the returned
  data.

# Raises

SSLError

: Any unexpected SSL error.

An SSL pipe.

Allows you to communicate with an SSL/TLS protocol instance through memory buffers. It can be used to implement a
security layer for an existing connection where you don't have access to the connection's file descriptor, or for
some reason you don't want to use it.

An `SSLPipe` can be in `wrapped` and `unwrapped` mode. In unwrapped mode, data is passed through untransformed.
In wrapped mode, application level data is encrypted to SSL record level data and vice versa. The SSL record level
is the lowest level in the SSL protocol suite and is what travels as-is over the wire.

An `SSLPipe` initially is in `unwrapped` mode. To start SSL, call `.do_handshake`, to shutdown SSL, call
`.unwrap`.

# Attributes

\_handshake_callback

: A Callback which will be called when handshake is completed. Set by `.do_handshake`.

  Should accept the following parameters:
  +-----------------------+---------------------------+
  | Respective name | Value |
  +=======================+===========================+
  | handshake_exception | `None`, `BaseException` |
  +-----------------------+---------------------------+

  If the handshake is successful, then the `handshake_exception` is given as `None`, else as an exception
  instance.

\_incoming

: Does the incoming data encryption/decryption.

\_need_ssl_data

: Whether more record level data is needed to complete a handshake that is currently in progress.

\_outgoing

: Does the outgoing data encryption/decryption.

\_server_host_name

: The ssl protocol's server hostname if applicable.

\_server_side

: Whether the ssl protocol is server side.

\_shutdown_callback

: A callback which will be called when the shutdown is completed. Set by `.shutdown`.

  Should accept no parameters.

\_ssl_context

: The SSL pipe's SSL type.

\_ssl_object

: SSL object connecting `._incoming` and `._outgoing` memory bios set at `.do_handshake`.

\_state

: The state of the `SSLPipe`.

  Can be set as one of:

  | Respective name             | Value |
  | --------------------------- | ----- |
  | SSL_PIPE_STATE_UNWRAPPED    | `0`   |
  | SSL_PIPE_STATE_DO_HANDSHAKE | `1`   |
  | SSL_PIPE_STATE_WRAPPED      | `2`   |
  | SSL_PIPE_STATE_SHUTDOWN     | `3`   |

  Note, that `.state` is checked by memory address and not by value.

classes.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_transport_layer.SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_transport_layer.SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer.__new__
documentation:
Creates a new ssl protocol layer.

# Parameters

loop

: The respective event loop of the protocol.

protocol

: Asynchronous protocol implementation.

ssl_context

: The connection ssl type. If SSl was given as `True` when creating a connection at this point we get it as
  `None`, so we create a default ssl context.

  Note, that if the connection is server side, a valid `ssl_context` should be given.

connection_made_waiter

: A waiter future, what's result is set when connection is made, aka handshake is completed, or if when the
  connection is lost, depending which happens first.

  After the future's result or exception is set, the attribute is set as `None`.

server_side

: Whether the ssl protocol is server side.

server_host_name

: The ssl protocol's server hostname if applicable.

  If we are the `server_side`, then this parameter is forced to `None` (wont raise).

call_connection_made

: Whether the the `protocol`'s `.connection_made` should be called when handshake is completed.

# Raises

ValueError

: If the protocol is server side, but `ssl_context` is not given.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_transport_layer.SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer.__repr__
documentation: Returns the ssl protocol transport's representation.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_transport_layer.SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer._wake_up_connection_made_waiter
documentation:
Wakes up the ssl protocol's `._connection_made_waiter` if applicable.

Called when connection is made closed or lost, depending, which happens first. If connection is made or closed,
then the connection made waiter's result is set as `None`, if the connection is lost, then the respective
exception is thrown into it if any.

# Parameters

exception

: Exception to throw into `._connection_made_waiter` if any.

Called when ssl handshake is completed.

    Exception occurred when processing backlog meanwhile handshaking.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_transport_layer.SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer._process_write_backlog
documentation:
Try to make progress on the write backlog.

Feeds data to `.ssl_pipe` till it is full.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_transport_layer.SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer._finalize
documentation:
Closes the ssl protocol's transport.

Called after shutdown or abortion.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.ssl_transport_layer.SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer.__del__
documentation: Closes the ssl protocol transport if not yet closed.

Asynchronous SSL protocol implementation on top of a `socket`. Uses `MemoryBIO`-s for incoming and
outgoing data.

# Attributes

\_extra

: Optional transport information.

\_loop

: The event loop to what the transport is bound to.

\_call_connection_made

: Whether the the `.app_protocol`'s `.connection_made` should be called when handshake is completed.

\_closing

: Whether the ssl protocol is shut or shutting down.

\_connection_made_waiter

: A waiter future, what's result is set when connection is made, aka handshake is completed, or if when the
  connection is lost, depending which happens first.

  After the future's result or exception is set, the attribute is set as `None`.

\_in_handshake

: Whether the ssl transport is in handshaking.

\_protocol

: Asynchronous protocol implementation.

\_server_host_name

: The ssl protocol's server hostname if applicable.

\_server_side

: Whether the ssl protocol is server side.

\_session_established

: Whether the session is established. Is set after handshake and is set back to `False` when the connection is
  lost.

\_ssl_context

: The connection's ssl type.

\_ssl_pipe

: Ssl pipe set meanwhile the protocol is connected to feed the ssl data to.

\_transport

: Asynchronous transport implementation.

\_write_backlog

: Ensured data queued up to be written. Each element contains a tuple of the data to write and an offset till
  write from.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.ensure_future
documentation:
Ensures the given coroutine or future on the event loop. Returns an awaitable `Future`.

# Parameters

coroutine_or_future

: The coroutine or future to ensure.

# Returns

future

: The return type depends on `coroutine_or_future`'s type.

  - If `coroutine_or_future` is given as `CoroutineType`, `GeneratorType`, returns a `Task`.
  - If `coroutine_or_future` is given as `Future`, bound to the current event loop, returns it.
  - If ``` coroutine_or_future`is given as ``Future` ```, bound to an other event loop, returns a
    : `FutureWrapperAsync`.
  - If `coroutine_or_future` defines an `__await__` method, then returns a `Task`.

# Raises

TypeError

: If `coroutine_or_future` is not `awaitable`.

documentation:
Ensures the given coroutine or future on the event loop. Returns an awaitable `Future`. Wakes up

: The coroutine or future to ensure.

# Returns

future

: The return type depends on `coroutine_or_future`'s type.

  - If `coroutine_or_future` is given as `CoroutineType`, `GeneratorType`, returns a `Task`.
  - If `coroutine_or_future` is given as `Future`, bound to the current event loop, returns it.
  - If ``` coroutine_or_future`is given as ``Future` ```, bound to an other event loop, returns a
    : `FutureWrapperAsync`.
  - If `coroutine_or_future` defines an `__await__` method, then returns a `Task`.

# Raises

TypeError

: If `coroutine_or_future` is not `awaitable`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._make_ssl_transport
documentation:
Creates an ssl transport with the given parameters.

# Parameters

socket

: The socket, what the transport will use.

protocol

: Asynchronous protocol implementation for the transport. The given protocol is wrapped into an
  `SSLBidirectionalTransportLayer`

ssl

: Ssl context of the respective connection.

waiter

: Waiter, what's result should be set, when the transport is ready to use.

server_side

: Whether the created ssl transport is a server side.

server_host_name

: Overwrites the hostname that the target server’s certificate will be matched against.
  By default the value of the host parameter is used. If host is empty, there is no default and you must pass
  a value for `server_host_name`. If `server_host_name` is an empty string, hostname matching is disabled
  (which is a serious security risk, allowing for potential man-in-the-middle attacks).

extra

: Optional transport information.

server

: The server to what the created socket will be attached to.

# Returns

transport

: The created ssl transport.

method as well for repeating itself after a a delay.)

: Factory function for creating an asynchronous compatible protocol.

socket

: The sockets to serve by the respective server if applicable.

ssl

: To enable ssl for the connections, give it as `SSLContext`.

server

: The respective server, what started to serve if applicable.

backlog

: The maximum number of queued connections passed to `socket.listen()`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._accept_connection
documentation:
Callback added by `._start_serving`, what is triggered by a read event. This method is only called once for
each event loop tick. There may be multiple connections waiting for an `.accept()` so it is called in a loop.
See `https://bugs.python.org/issue27906` for more details.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Factory function for creating an asynchronous compatible protocol.

socket

: The sockets to serve by the respective server if applicable.

ssl

: The ssl type of the connection if any.

server

: The respective server if applicable.

backlog

: The maximum number of queued connections passed to `socket.listen()`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._accept_connection_task
documentation:
Called by `._accept_connection`, when a connection is accepted.

Because `._accept_connection` might have more connections to accept, multiple tasks are launched up from
this method to run parallelly.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Factory function for creating an asynchronous compatible protocol.

connection_socket

: The accepted connection.

extra

: Optional transport information.

ssl

: The ssl type of the connection if any.

server

: The respective server if applicable.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.connect_accepted_socket
documentation:
Wrap an already accepted connection into a transport/protocol pair.

This method can be used by servers that accept connections outside of hata but use it to handle them.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Callable returning an asynchronous protocol implementation.

socket

: A preexisting socket object returned from `socket.accept`.

ssl

: Whether ssl should be enabled.

# Returns

transport

: The created transport. If `ssl` is enabled, creates `SSLBidirectionalTransportLayerTransport`, else
  `SocketTransportLayer`.

protocol

: The protocol returned by `protocol_factory`.

# Raises

ValueError

: If `socket` is not a stream socket.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._create_connection_shared_precheck
documentation:
Shared precheck by `.create_connection_to` and by `.create_connection_with`.

# Parameters

ssl

: Whether ssl should be enabled.

server_host_name

: Overwrites the host name that the target server’s certificate will be matched against.
  Should only be passed if `ssl` is not `None`. By default the value of the host parameter is used. If host
  is empty, there is no default and you must pass a value for `server_host_name`. If `server_host_name` is an
  empty string, host name matching is disabled (which is a serious security risk, allowing for potential
  man-in-the-middle attacks).

host

: To what network interfaces should the connection be bound.

# Returns

ssl : None\`, `SSLContext`
server_host_name : `None`, `str`

# Raises

ValueError
: - If `server_host_name` is not set, meanwhile using `ssl` without `host`.
  - If `server_host_name` is set, but `ssl` is.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_connection_to
documentation:
Open a streaming transport connection to a given address specified by `host` and `port`.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Callable returning an asynchronous protocol implementation.

host

: To what network interfaces should the connection be bound.

port

: The port of the `host`.

ssl

: Whether ssl should be enabled.

socket_family

: Can be either `AF_INET`, `AF_INET6`, `AF_UNIX`.

socket_protocol

: Can be used to narrow host resolution. Is passed to `.get_address_info`.

socket_flags

: Can be used to narrow host resolution. Is passed to `.get_address_info`.

local_address

: Can be given as a `tuple` (`local_host`, `local_port`) to bind the socket locally. The `local_host` and
  `local_port` are looked up by `.get_address_info`.

server_host_name

: Overwrites the hostname that the target server’s certificate will be matched against.
  By default the value of the host parameter is used. If host is empty, there is no default and you must pass
  a value for `server_host_name`. If `server_host_name` is an empty string, hostname matching is disabled
  (which is a serious security risk, allowing for potential man-in-the-middle attacks).

# Raises

ValueError
: - If `server_host_name` is not set, meanwhile using `ssl` without `host`.
  - If `server_host_name` is set, but `ssl` is.

OSError
: - `get_address_info()` returned empty list.
  - Error while attempting to bind to address.
  - Cannot open connection to any address.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_connection_with
documentation:
Opens a stream transport connection to the given `socket`.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

socket

: Whether should use an existing, already connected socket.

ssl

: Whether ssl should be enabled.

server_host_name

: Overwrites the host name that the target server’s certificate will be matched against.
  Should only be passed if `ssl` is not `None`. By default the value of the host parameter is used. If host
  is empty, there is no default and you must pass a value for `server_host_name`. If `server_host_name` is an
  empty string, host name matching is disabled (which is a serious security risk, allowing for potential
  man-in-the-middle attacks).

# Returns

protocol

: The protocol returned by `protocol_factory`.

# Raises

ValueError
: - If `server_host_name` is not set, meanwhile using `ssl` without `host`.
  - If `server_host_name` is set, but `ssl` is.

OSError
: - `get_address_info()` returned empty list.
  - Error while attempting to bind to address.
  - Cannot open connection to any address.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._create_connection_transport
documentation:
Open a streaming transport connection to a given address specified by `host` and `port`.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Callable returning an asynchronous protocol implementation.

ssl

: Whether ssl should be enabled.

socket

: The socket to what the created transport should be connected to.

server_host_name

: Overwrites the hostname that the target server’s certificate will be matched against.
  Should only be passed if `ssl` is not `None`. By default the value of the host parameter is used. If host
  is empty, there is no default and you must pass a value for `server_host_name`. If `server_host_name` is an
  empty string, hostname matching is disabled (which is a serious security risk, allowing for potential
  man-in-the-middle attacks).

server_side

: Whether the server or the client creates the connection transport.

# Returns

protocol

: The protocol returned by `protocol_factory`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._create_unix_connection_shared_precheck
documentation:
Shared precheck used by ``` .create_unix_connection_to` and by ``.create_unix_connection_with ```.

# Parameters

ssl

: Whether ssl should be enabled.

server_host_name

: Overwrites the host name that the target server’s certificate will be matched against.
  Should only be passed if `ssl` is not `None`.

# Returns

ssl : None\`, `SSLContext`
server_host_name : `None`, `str`

# Raises

> - If `server_host_name` parameter is given, but `ssl` isn't.
> - If `ssl` parameter is given, but `server_host_name` is not.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_unix_connection_to
documentation:
Establish a unix socket connection.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Callable returning an asynchronous protocol implementation.

path

: The path to open connection to.

ssl

: Whether ssl should be enabled.

server_host_name

: Overwrites the hostname that the target server’s certificate will be matched against.
  Should only be passed if `ssl` is not `None`. By default the value of the host parameter is used. If hos
  is empty, there is no default and you must pass a value for `server_host_name`. If `server_host_name` is an
  empty string, hostname matching is disabled (which is a serious security risk, allowing for potential
  man-in-the-middle attacks).

# Returns

transport

: The created transport. If `ssl` is enabled, creates `SSLBidirectionalTransportLayerTransport`, else
  `SocketTransportLayer`.

protocol

: The protocol returned by `protocol_factory`.

# Raises

ValueError
: - If `server_host_name` parameter is given, but `ssl` isn't.
  - If `ssl` parameter is given, but `server_host_name` is not.

NotImplementedError

: Not supported on windows by the library.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_unix_connection_with

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.get_address_info
documentation:
Asynchronous version of `socket.getaddrinfo()`.

# Parameters

host

: To respective network interface.

port

: The port of the `host`.

family

: The address family.

type

: Socket type.

protocol

: Protocol type. Can be used to narrow host resolution.

flags

: Can be used to narrow host resolution.

# Returns

future

: An awaitable future, what will yield the lookup's result.

  Might raise `OSError` or return a `list` of `tuple`-s with the following elements:
  \- `family` : `AddressFamily`, `int`. Address family.
  \- `type` : `SocketKind`, `int`. Socket type.
  \- `protocol` : `int`. Protocol type.
  \- `canonical_name` : `str`. Represents the canonical name of the host.
  \- `socket_address` : `tuple` (`` str, `int ``). Socket address containing the `host` and the `port`.
await it

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.get_name_info
documentation:
Asynchronous version of `socket.getnameinfo()`.

# Parameters

socket_address

: Socket address as a tuple of `host` and `port`.

flags

: Can be used to narrow host resolution.

# Returns

future

: An awaitable future, what will yield the lookup's result.
await it

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._ensure_resolved
documentation:
Ensures, that the given address is already a resolved IP. If not, gets it's address.

# Parameters

address

: Address as a tuple of `host` and `port`.

family

: The address family.

type

: Socket type.

protocol

: Protocol type. Can be used to narrow host resolution.

flags

: Can be used to narrow host resolution.

# Returns

future

: An awaitable future, what returns a `list` of `tuples` with the following elements:

  - `family` : `AddressFamily`, `int`. Address family.
  - `type` : `SocketKind`, `int`. Socket type.
  - `protocol` : `int`. Protocol type.
  - `canonical_name` : `str`. Represents the canonical name of the host.
  - `socket_address` : `tuple` (`` str, `int ``). Socket address containing the `host` and the `port`.

  Might raise `OSError` as well.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.socket_accept
documentation:
Accept a connection. Modeled after the blocking `socket.accept()` method.

The socket must be bound to an address and listening for connections.

This method is a coroutine.

Parameters
----------
socket : `socket.socket`
    Must be a non-blocking socket.

Returns
-------
conn : `socket.socket`
    The connected socket.
address : `tuple` (`str`, `int`)
    The address to what the connection is connected to.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._socket_accept
documentation:
Method used by `.socket_accept` to check whether the respective socket can be accepted already.

If the respective socket is already connected, then sets the waiter future's result instantly, else adds itself
as a reader callback.

# Parameters

future

: Waiter future, what's result or exception is set, when the socket can be accepted or when an exception
  occurs.

socket

: The respective socket, what's is listening for a connection.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.socket_connect
documentation:
Connect the given socket to a remote socket at address.

Asynchronous version of `socket.connect`.

This method is a coroutine.

Parameters
----------
socket : `socket.socket`
    Must be a non-blocking socket.
address : `tuple` (`str`, `int`)
    The address to connect to.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._socket_connect_callback
documentation:
Reader callback, what is called, when the respective socket is connected. Added by `.socket_connect`, when the
socket is not yet connected.

# Parameters

future

: Waiter future, what's result is set, when the socket is connected.

socket

: The respective socket, on what's connection we are waiting for.

address

: The address to connect to.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.socket_receive
documentation:
Receive up to `number_of_bytes` from the given socket. Asynchronous version of `socket.recv()`.

This method is a coroutine.

Parameters
----------
socket : `socket.socket`
    The socket to receive the data from. Must be a non-blocking socket.
number_of_bytes : `int`
    The amount of data to receive in bytes.

Returns
-------
data : `bytes`
    The received data.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._socket_receive
documentation:
Added reader callback by `.socket_receive`. This method is repeated till the data is successfully polled.

# Parameters

future

: Waiter future, what's result or exception will be set.

socket

: The socket from what we read.

number_of_bytes

: The amount of data to receive in bytes.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.socket_receive_into
documentation:
Receive data from the socket and stores it into the given buffer. Asynchronous version of `socket.recv_into()`.

This method is a coroutine.

Parameters
----------
socket : `socket.socket`
    The socket to receive the data from. Must be a non-blocking socket.
buffer : `object`
    Buffer to receive the data into. Can be `bytearray` for example.
    
Returns
-------
number_of_bytes : `int`
    The number of bytes received.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._socket_receive_into
documentation:
Added reader callback by `.socket_receive_into`. This method is repeated till data is successfully polled.

# Parameters

future

: Waiter future, what's result or exception will be set.

socket

: The socket from what we read.

buffer

: Buffer to receive the data into. Can be `bytearray` for example.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.socket_send_all
documentation:
Send data to the socket. Asynchronous version of `socket.sendall`.

Continues sending to the socket until all data is sent or an error occurs.

This method is a coroutine.

Parameters
----------
socket : `socket.socket`
    The socket to send the data to. Must be a non-blocking socket.
data : `bytes-like`
    The data to send.

Notes
-----
There is no way to determine how much data, if any was successfully received on the other end of the connection.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._socket_send_all
documentation:
Added writer callback by `.socket_send_all`. This method is repeated till the whole data is exhausted.

# Parameters

future

: Waiter future, what's result or exception will be set.

socket

: The socket to what the data is sent to.

data

: Memoryview on the data to send.

start_reference

: Reference to the start index.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._socket_write_done_callback
documentation:
Callback added to the waited future by `socket_connect` to remove the respective socket from the
writers by it's file descriptor.

The `file_descriptor` and `handle` parameters are passed initially when the callback is created.

# Attributes

file_descriptor

: The respective socket's file descriptor's identifier.

handle

: The Handle ran when the socket is ready to be read.

future

: The respective future what's result is set when the socket connected successfully.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._socket_read_done_callback
documentation:
Done callback of `.socket_receive_into` and `.socket_receive_into`. Called when the waiter future is done.

The `file_descriptor` and `handle` parameters are passed initially when the callback is created.

# Parameters

file_descriptor

: The respective file descriptor.

handle

: Reader callback.

future

: The respective future what's result is set when data is read successfully.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._create_datagram_connection
documentation:
Creates a datagram connection.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Factory function for creating a protocols.

socket

: Socket to bind the datagram transport to.

address

: The last address, where the transport sent data. Defaults to `None`. The send target address should not
  differ from the last, where the transport sent data.

# Returns

protocol

: The protocol returned by `protocol_factory`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_datagram_connection_to
documentation:
Creates a datagram connection. The socket type will be `SOCK_DGRAM`.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Factory function for creating a protocols.

local_address

: Can be given as a `tuple` (`local_host`, `local_port`) to bind the socket locally. The `local_host` and
  `local_port` are looked up by `.get_address_info`.

  If `socket_family` is given as `AF_UNIX`, then also can be path of a file or a file descriptor.

remote_address

: Can be given as a `tuple` (`remote_host`, `remote_port`) to connect the socket to remove address. The
  `remote_host` and `remote_port` are looked up by `.get_address_info`.

  If `socket_family` is given as `AF_UNIX`, then also can be path of a file or a file descriptor.

socket_family

: Can be either `AF_INET`, `AF_INET6`, `AF_UNIX`.

socket_protocol

: Can be used to narrow host resolution. Is passed to `.get_address_info`.

socket_flags

: Can be used to narrow host resolution. Is passed to `.get_address_info`.

reuse_port

: Tells to the kernel to allow this endpoint to be bound to the same port as an other existing endpoint
  already might be bound to.

  Not supported on Windows.

allow_broadcast

: Tells the kernel to allow this endpoint to send messages to the broadcast address.

# Returns

protocol

: The protocol returned by `protocol_factory`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_datagram_connection_with
documentation:
Creates a datagram connection. The socket type will be `SOCK_DGRAM`.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Factory function for creating a protocols.

socket

# Returns

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._create_server_get_address_info
documentation:
Gets address info for the given parameters. This method is used by `.create_server`, when resolving hosts.

# Parameters

host

: Network interfaces should the server be bound.

port

: The port to use by the `host`.

socket_family

: The family of the address.

socket_flags

: Bit-mask for `get_address_info`.

# Returns

future

: A future, what's result is set, when the address is dispatched.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread._create_server_shared_precheck
documentation:
Shared precheck used by `.create_server` and by `.create_server`.

# Parameters

ssl

: Whether and what ssl is enabled for the connections.

# Raises

TypeError
: - If `ssl` is not given either as `None`, `SSLContext`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_server_to
documentation:
Creates a TCP server (socket type SOCK_STREAM) listening on port of the host address.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Factory function for creating a protocols.

host

: To what network interfaces should the server be bound.

port

: The port to use by the host\`(s).

socket_family

: Can be given either as `socket.AF_INET`, `socket.AF_INET6` to force the socket to use `IPv4`, `IPv6`.
  If not given, then will be determined from host name.

socket_flags

: Bit-mask for `get_address_info`.

backlog

: The maximum number of queued connections passed to `socket.listen()`.

ssl

: Whether and what ssl is enabled for the connections.

reuse_address

: Tells the kernel to reuse a local socket in `TIME_WAIT` state, without waiting for its natural timeout to
  expire. If not specified will automatically be set to True on Unix.

reuse_port

: Tells to the kernel to allow this endpoint to be bound to the same port as an other existing endpoint
  already might be bound to.

  Not supported on Windows.

# Returns

server

: The created server instance.

# Raises

TypeError
: - If `ssl` is not given either as `None`, `SSLContext`.
  - If `reuse_port` is given as non `bool`.
  - If `reuse_address` is given as non `bool`.
  - If `reuse_port` is given as non `bool`.
  - If `host` is not given as `None`, `str` and neither as `iterable` of `None`, `str`.

ValueError
: - If `reuse_port` is given as `True`, but not supported.

OsError

: Error while attempting to binding to address.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.create_server_with
documentation:
Creates a TCP server (socket type SOCK_STREAM) listening on port of the host address.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

protocol_factory

: Factory function for creating a protocols.

socket

: Can be specified in order to use a preexisting socket object.

backlog

: The maximum number of queued connections passed to `socket.listen()`.

ssl

: Whether and what ssl is enabled for the connections.

# Returns

server

: The created server instance.

# Raises

TypeError
: - If `ssl` is not given either as `None`, `SSLContext`.

ValueError
: - If `socket` is given, but it's type is not `module_socket.SOCK_STREAM`.

OsError

: Error while attempting to binding to address.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.connect_read_pipe
documentation:
Register the read end of the given pipe in the event loop.

This method is a coroutine.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.subprocess_shell
documentation:
Create a subprocess from cmd.

This is similar to the standard library `subprocess.Popen` class called with `shell = True`.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

cmd

: The command to execute. Should use the platform’s “shell” syntax.

stdin

: Standard input for the created shell

stdout

: Standard output for the created shell.

stderr

: Standard error for the created shell

extra

: Optional transport information.

preexecution_function

: This object is called in the child process just before the child is executed. POSIX only, defaults to
  `None`.

close_fds

: Defaults to `True`

  If `close_fds` is True, all file descriptors except `0`, `1` and `2` will be closed before the child
  process is executed. Otherwise when `close_fds` is False, file descriptors obey their inheritable flag as
  described in Inheritance of File Descriptors.

cwd

: The current working directory.

  If `cwd` is not `None`, the function changes the working directory to cwd before executing the child.

startup_info

: Is passed to the underlying `CreateProcess` function.

creation_flags

: Can be given as 1 of the following flags:

  - `CREATE_NEW_CONSOLE`
  - `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP`
  - `ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `REALTIME_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `CREATE_NO_WINDOW`
  - `DETACHED_PROCESS`
  - `CREATE_DEFAULT_ERROR_MODE`
  - `CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB`

restore_signals

: If given as `True`, so by default, all signals that Python has set to `SIG_IGN` are restored to `SIG_DFL`
  in the child process before the exec. Currently this includes the `SIGPIPE`, `SIGXFZ` and `SIGXFSZ`
  signals. POSIX only.

start_new_session

: If given as `True` the `setsid()` system call will be made in the child process prior to the execution of
  the subprocess. POSIX only, defaults to `False`.

pass_fds

: An optional sequence of file descriptors to keep open between the parent and the child. Providing any
  `pass_fds` forces `close_fds` to be `True`. POSIX only, defaults to empty tuple.

process_open_keyword_parameters

: Additional parameters to pass to the `Popen`.

# Returns

process : `AsyncProcess`

# Raises

TypeError

: If `cmd` is not given as `str` not `bytes` object.

NotImplementedError

: Not supported on windows by the library.

methods.name: scarletio.core.event_loop.event_loop.EventThread.subprocess_exec
documentation:
Create a subprocess from one or more string parameters specified by args.

This is similar to the standard library `subprocess.Popen` class called with `shell = False`.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

program

: The program executable.

args

: Parameters to open the `program` with.

stdin

: Standard input for the created shell.

stdout

: Standard output for the created shell.

stderr

: > , Optional (Keyword only)

  Standard error for the created shell.

extra

: Optional transport information.

preexecution_function

: This object is called in the child process just before the child is executed. POSIX only, defaults to
  `None`.

close_fds

: Defaults to `True`

  If `close_fds` is True, all file descriptors except `0`, `1` and `2` will be closed before the child
  process is executed. Otherwise when `close_fds` is False, file descriptors obey their inheritable flag as
  described in Inheritance of File Descriptors.

cwd

: The current working directory.

  If `cwd` is not `None`, the function changes the working directory to cwd before executing the child.

startup_info

: Is passed to the underlying `CreateProcess` function.

creation_flags

: Can be given as 1 of the following flags:

  - `CREATE_NEW_CONSOLE`
  - `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP`
  - `ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `REALTIME_PRIORITY_CLASS`
  - `CREATE_NO_WINDOW`
  - `DETACHED_PROCESS`
  - `CREATE_DEFAULT_ERROR_MODE`
  - `CREATE_BREAKAWAY_FROM_JOB`

restore_signals

: If given as `True`, so by default, all signals that Python has set to `SIG_IGN` are restored to `SIG_DFL`
  in the child process before the exec. Currently this includes the `SIGPIPE`, `SIGXFZ` and `SIGXFSZ`
  signals. POSIX only.

start_new_session

: If given as `True` the `setsid()` system call will be made in the child process prior to the execution of
  the subprocess. POSIX only, defaults to `False`.

pass_fds

: An optional sequence of file descriptors to keep open between the parent and the child. Providing any
  `pass_fds` forces `close_fds` to be `True`. POSIX only, defaults to empty tuple.

process_open_keyword_parameters

: Additional parameters to pass to the `Popen`.

# Returns

process : `AsyncProcess`

# Raises

NotImplementedError

: Not supported on windows by the library.

Event loops run asynchronous tasks and callbacks, perform network IO operations, and runs subprocesses.

At hata event loops are represented by `EventThread`-s, which do not block the source thread, as they say, they
use their own thread for it.

# Attributes

\_kept_executor_count

: The minimal amount of executors to keep alive (or not close).

\_kept_executor_last_schedule

: When was last time cleanup scheduled.

\_kept_executor_release_handle

: Executor release handle.

claimed_executors

: Claimed executors, which are given back to the executor on release.

free_executors

: The free (or not used) executors of the executor.

running_executors

: The executors under use.

\_async_generators: `WeakSet` of `async_generator`

: The asynchronous generators bound to the event loop.

\_async_generators_shutdown_called

: Whether the event loop's asynchronous generators where shut down.

\_self_write_socket

: Socket, which can be used to wake up the thread by writing into it.

\_ready

: Ready to run handles of the event loop.

\_scheduled

: Scheduled timer handles, which will be moved to `._ready` when their `when` becomes lower or equal to the
  respective loop time.

\_self_read_socket

: Socket, which reads from `._self_write_socket`.

context

: Context of the event loop to ensure it's safe startup and closing.

current_task

: The actually running task of the event thread. Set only meanwhile a task is executed.

running

: Whether the event loop is running.

selector

: Selector to poll from socket.

should_run

: Whether the event loop should do more loops.

started

: Whether the event loop was already started.

# Notes

Event threads support weakreferencing and dynamic attribute names as well.

documentation:
Returns the low and the high water of the transport.

# Returns

low_water

: The `.protocol` is resumed writing when the buffer size goes under the low water mark. Defaults to
  `16384`.

high_water

: The `.protocol` is paused writing when the buffer size passes the high water mark. Defaults to
  `65536`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.abstract.AbstractTransportLayerBase.set_write_buffer_limits
documentation:
Set the high- and low-water limits for write flow control.

These two values control when to call the protocol's `.pause_writing` and `.resume_writing` methods. If
specified, the low-water limit must be less than or equal to the high-water limit. Neither value can be
negative. The defaults are implementation-specific. If only the high-water limit is given, the low-water limit
defaults to an implementation-specific value less than or equal to the high-water limit. Setting high to zero
forces low to zero as well, and causes `.pause_writing` to be called whenever the buffer becomes non-empty.
Setting low to zero causes `.resume_writing` to be called only once the buffer is empty. Use of zero for
either limit is generally sub-optimal as it reduces opportunities for doing I/O and computation concurrently.

# Parameters

high

: High limit to stop reading if reached.

low

: Low limit to start reading if reached.

# Raises

ValueError

: If `low` is lower than `0` or if `low` is higher than `high`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.abstract.AbstractTransportLayerBase.pause_reading
documentation:
Pauses the receiving end.

No data will be passed to the respective protocol's `.data_received` method until `.resume_reading` is
called.

# Returns

reading_paused

: Whether reading was paused.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.abstract.AbstractTransportLayerBase.resume_reading
documentation:
Resumes the receiving end.

Data received will once again be passed to the respective protocol's `.data_received` method.

# Returns

    The catched exception.

Defines abstract protocol functionality.

# Structure

All protocol should implement the following methods:
\- `.connection_made`
\- `.connection_lost`
\- `.close`
\- `.close_transport`
\- `.get_transport`
\- `.get_extra_info`

Read protocols should implement the following:
\- `.set_exception`
\- `.eof_received`
\- `.data_received`

Write protocols should implement the following:
\- `.pause_writing`
\- `.resume_writing`
\- `.write`
\- `.writelines`
\- `.write_eof`
\- `.can_write_eof`
\- `.drain`

Datagram protocols should implement the following:
\- `.datagram_received`
\- `.error_received`

documentation:
Sets the write buffer limits of the transport.

# Parameters

low

: The `.protocol` is paused writing when the buffer size passes the high water mark. Defaults to `65536`.

high

: The `.protocol` is resumed writing when the buffer size goes under the low water mark. Defaults to
  `16384`.

# Raises

ValueError

: If `high` is lower than `low` or if `low` is lower than `0`.

Asynchronous write only transport implementation for pipes.

# Attributes

\_buffer

: Data ensured to be written on the wrapped pipe as it becomes readable again.

\_extra

: Optional transport information.

\_high_water

: The `.protocol` is paused writing when the buffer size passes the high water mark. Defaults to `65536`.

\_low_water

: The `.protocol` is resumed writing when the buffer size goes under the low water mark. Defaults to `16384`.

\_closing

: Whether the transport ic closing.

\_file_descriptor

: The used socket's file descriptor number.

\_loop

: The respective event loop of the transport.

\_pipe

: The pipe to connect to on read end.

  Is set to non-blocking mode.

  After closing the transport is set to `None`.

\_protocol

: Asynchronous protocol implementation used by the transport.

  After closing the transport is set to `None`.

\_protocol_paused

: Whether `.protocol` is paused writing.

functions.name: scarletio.utils.trace.frame_grouping._merge_frame_groups
documentation:
Merges the given frame groups to produce bigger non-repeat frame groups.
Call it after creating frame groups to merge too short to be repeated frame groups into single mention groups.
Call also after separating repeats to merge the cut down non-repeat parts.

    The frame groups to merge.

functions.name: scarletio.utils.trace.frame_grouping._separate_repeats_in_frame_groups
documentation:
Separates the repeated parts down. Each frame may produce `itself` or `before - middle (repeated) - after` parts.
Repeat separating is then again applied to the `before` and `after` parts till every frame group is either
marked as single-mention or repeat.

Using a flattened algorithm to avoid recursion.

Parameters
----------
frame_groups : `list<frameGroup>`
    The frame groups to merge.

Returns

frame_groups : `list<FrameGroup>`

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.protocol.ReadProtocolBase._read_once
documentation:
Reader task for reading exactly one chunk out.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

payload_stream

: Payload buffer to read into.

Asynchronous read protocol implementation.

Scarlet-io backend uses optimistic generator based chunked readers, which have really long and complicated
implementation, but their speed is pretty good.

# Attributes

\_at_eof

: Whether the protocol received end of file.

\_chunks

: Right feed, left pop queue, used to store the received data chunks.

\_exception

: Exception set by `.set_exception`, when an unexpected exception occur meanwhile reading from socket.

\_loop

: The event loop to what the protocol is bound to.

\_offset

: Byte offset, of the used up data of the most-left chunk.

\_paused

: Whether the protocol's respective transport's reading is paused. Defaults to `False`.

  Also note, that not every transport supports pausing.

\_payload_stream

: Payload stream of the protocol.

\_payload_reader

: Payload reader generator, what gets the control back, when data, eof or any exception is received.

\_transport

: Asynchronous transport implementation. Is set meanwhile the protocol is alive.

# Attributes

\_at_eof

\_chunks

: Right feed, left pop queue, used to store the received data chunks.

\_exception

\_loop

\_offset

: Byte offset, of the used up data of the most-left chunk.

\_paused

: Whether the protocol's respective transport's reading is paused. Defaults to `False`.

  Also note, that not every transport supports pausing.

\_payload_reader

: Payload reader generator, what gets the control back, when data, eof or any exception is received.

\_payload_stream

: Payload stream of the protocol.

\_transport

: Asynchronous transport implementation. Is set meanwhile the protocol is alive.

\_drain_waiter

: A future, what is used to block the writing task, till it's writen data is drained.

classes.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.protocol.DatagramAddressedReadProtocol

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.protocol.DatagramAddressedReadProtocol.__new__
documentation:
Creates a new datagram addressed read protocol instance bound to the given loop.

# Parameters

loop

: The loop to what the protocol gonna be bound to.

protocol_factory

: Protocol type to create for each address.

  Defaults to `ReadProtocolBase`.

methods.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.protocol.DatagramAddressedReadProtocol.wait_for_receive
documentation:
Can be used to wait for payload to receive. Note, that this method should be used only initially, because the
reader protocols implement the reading.

This method is a coroutine.

# Parameters

address

: The address of which payload is waiter for.

timeout

: The maximal amount of time to wait before raising `TimeoutError`.

# Returns

result
: - If `timeout` is given and timeout occur, then returns `None`.
  - if `address` is given and data is received from ir, then returns the respective `ReadProtocolBase`.
  - If `address` is not given, then returns a `tuple` of the respective `address` and protocol.

# Raises

TimeoutError
: - If timeout occurred.

Datagram reader protocol for reading from payloads form multiple addresses.

# Attributes

\_by_address

: Dictionary to store the alive readers by address.

\_loop

: The loop to what the protocol is bound to.

\_protocol_factory

: Protocol type to create for each address.

\_transport

: Asynchronous transport implementation, what calls the protocol's `.datagram_received` when data is
  received.

\_waiters

: Waiters for any payload receive if applicable.

classes.name: scarletio.core.protocols_and_transports.protocol.DatagramMergerReadProtocol
documentation:
Asynchronous read protocol implementation.

Scarlet-io backend uses optimistic generator based chunked readers, which have really long and complicated
implementation, but their speed is pretty good.

# Attributes

\_at_eof

: Whether the protocol received end of file.

\_chunks

: Right feed, left pop queue, used to store the received data chunks.

\_exception

: Exception set by `.set_exception`, when an unexpected exception occur meanwhile reading from socket.

\_loop

: The event loop to what the protocol is bound to.

\_offset

: Byte offset, of the used up data of the most-left chunk.

\_paused

: Whether the protocol's respective transport's reading is paused. Defaults to `False`.

  Also note, that not every transport supports pausing.

\_payload_reader

: Payload reader generator, what gets the control back, when data, eof or any exception is received.

\_payload_stream

: Payload waiter of the protocol, what's result is set, when the `.payload_reader` generator returns.

  If cancelled or marked by done or any other methods, the payload reader will not be cancelled.

\_transport

: Asynchronous transport implementation. Is set meanwhile the protocol is alive.

    The line where the syntax is invalid. Can be empty string.

    The line's index in the file.

    The represented exception's type's name.

functions.name: scarletio.utils.trace.exception_representation.suggestion.helpers_familiarity._split_part_by_numbers
documentation:
Splits the given string part by numbers.

This function is an iterable generator.

Examples
--------
```py
325 -> 325
koishi -> koishi
koishi3 -> koishi, 3
3koishi -> 3, koishi
46koishi12satori -> 46, satori, 12, satori
```

Parameters
----------
part : `str`
    String part.

Yields
------
part : `str`

functions.name: scarletio.utils.trace.exception_representation.suggestion.helpers_familiarity._split_part_by_casing
documentation:
Splits the given string parts by casing.

This function is an iterable generator.

Examples
--------
```py
koishi -> koishi
KOISHI -> KOISHI
Koishi -> Koishi
koishiSatori -> koishi, Satori
KOISHISatori -> KOISHI, Satori
koishiSATORI -> koishi, SATORI
KOISHIsatori -> KOISH, Isatori # There is a limit how much we can do
```

Parameters
----------
part : `str`
    String part.

Yields
------
part : `str`

functions.name: scarletio.utils.trace.exception_representation.suggestion.helpers_familiarity._iter_split_name_to_words
documentation:
Splits the given name to words.

This function is an iterable generator.

Examples
--------
```py
koishi_satori_69 -> koishi, satori, 69
KoishiSatori69 -> Koishi, Satori, 69
KOISHI_SATORI_69 -> KOISHI, Satori, 69
KOISHISATORI69 -> KOISHISATORI, 69 # there is a limit how much we can do
```

Parameters
----------
name : `str`
    The name to split.

Yields
------
word : `str`

functions.name: scarletio.utils.trace.exception_representation.syntax_error_helpers._are_details_valid
documentation:
Checks whether the syntax error details are valid.

Parameters
----------
details : `(object, object, object, object) | (object, object, object, object, object, object)`
    A tuple of length 4 or 6.

Returns
-------
details_valid : `bool`
Pulled from C 3.11
args = Py_BuildValue("(O(OiiNii))", errmsg, tok->filename, tok->lineno,
col_offset, errtext, tok->lineno, end_col_offset);
if (args) {
PyErr_SetObject(PyExc_SyntaxError, args);
file_name is annotated as `O`, so it probably can be `None` as well.
also: line_number == end_line_number, so we can ignore it.
On older Python versions `line` can also be `None`. How stupid!
At some cases exception is built differently tho:
PyErr_Format(PyExc_SyntaxError, DUPLICATE_ARGUMENT, name);
SET_ERROR_LOCATION(st->st_filename, loc);
Here the exception's details are omitted instead they are set as slots only.
Because we do not use the slots instead pull the details from its parameters, we have to fix exception up.

classes.name: scarletio.utils.ignore_case_string.IgnoreCaseString

methods.name: scarletio.utils.ignore_case_string.IgnoreCaseString.__new__
documentation:
Return an string which ignores casing. If object is not provided, returns the empty string. Otherwise, the
behavior of `IgnoreCaseString` depends on whether encoding or errors is given, as follows.

If neither encoding nor errors is given, `IgnoreCaseString(object)` returns `object.__str__()`, which is the
"informal" or nicely printable string representation of object. For string objects, this is `string`. If
object does not have a `__str__()` method, then `IgnoreCaseString()` falls back to returning `repr(object)`.

If at least one of encoding or errors is given, object should be a `bytes-like` object. In this case, if object
is a `byte-like` object, then `IgnoreCaseString(bytes, encoding, errors)` is equivalent to
`bytes.decode(encoding, errors)`. Otherwise, the bytes object underlying the buffer object is obtained before
calling `bytes.decode()`.

Passing a `bytes-like` object to `IgnoreCaseString` without the encoding or errors parameters falls under the
first case of returning the informal string representation.

# Parameters

value

: The value, what is representation or encoded version is returned.

encoding

: Encoding to use when decoding a `bytes-like`.

errors

: May be given to set a different error handling scheme when decoding from `bytes-like`. The default `errors`
  value is `'strict'`, meaning that encoding errors raise a `UnicodeError`. Other possible values are
  `'ignore'`, `'replace'`, `'xmlcharrefreplace'`, `'backslashreplace'` and any other name registered via
  `codecs.register_error()`.

# Returns

self : `IgnoreCaseString`

documentation: Returns the string's hash value.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.hybrid_value_dictionary.HybridValueDictionary.update
documentation:
Updates the hybrid value dictionary with the given iterable's elements.

Parameters
----------
iterable : `iterable`
    Iterable to extend the hybrid value dictionary with.
    
    Can be given as an object, which:
    - supports `.items` iterator.
    - supports `.keys` and `.__getitem__`.
    - is `iterable` and each iteration returns a sequence with 2 elements.

Raises
------
TypeError
    The given `iterable` is not `iterable`.
ValueError
    The the given `iterable` sot supports neither `.items` or (`.keys` and `.__getitem__`) and one of it's
    element's length is not `2`.

value_iterator : ``_HybridValueDictionaryValueIterator``

\_iterating

: Whether the hybrid value dictionary is iterating and how much times.

\_callback

: Callback added to the `HybridValueDictionary`'s weak elements.

# Class Attributes

MAX_REPR_ELEMENT_LIMIT

: The maximal amount of items to render by `.__repr__`.

# Notes

`HybridValueDictionary`-s are weakreferable.

classes.name: scarletio.utils.keep_type.KeepType

methods.name: scarletio.utils.keep_type.KeepType.__new__
documentation:
Creates a new `KeepType` with given `old_class` to extend. Can be used as a decorator if `new_class`
parameter is not given.

# Parameters

old_class

: The old class to extend.

new_class

: The new class to extend the old class's functionality with.

# Returns

obj

: If only `old_class` attribute is given, then returns itself enabling using it as a decorator, but if
  `new_class` is given as well, then returns the extended `old_class`.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.keep_type.KeepType.__call__
documentation:
Calls the `KeepType` extending it's old `.old_class` with the new given `new_class`.

# Parameters

new_class

: The new class to extend the old class's functionality with.

# Returns

old_class

: The extended old class.

A decorator, what can be used to add features to an already existing class, by defining a new one, what will extend
the old one's functionality.

Note, that already existing attributes will not be overwritten and neither of the followingly named attributes
either:
    - `__name__`
    - `__qualname__`
    - `__weakref__`
    - `__dict__`
    - `__slots__`

Attributes
----------
old_class : `type`
    The old class to extend.

Class Attributes
----------------
_ignored_attr_names : `set` of `str`
    Attribute names to ignore when extending.

----------

    The key to match.

    Default value to set and return if `key` is not present in the weak value dictionary.

    The matched value, or `default` if none.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.weak_value_dictionary.WeakValueDictionary.update
documentation:
Updates the weak value dictionary with the given iterable's elements.

Parameters
----------
iterable : `iterable`
    Iterable to extend the weak value dictionary with.
    
    Can be given as an object, which:
    - supports `.items` iterator.
    - supports `.keys` and `.__getitem__`.
    - is `iterable` and each iteration returns a sequence with 2 elements.

Raises
------
TypeError
    The given `iterable` is not `iterable`.
ValueError
    The the given `iterable` sot supports neither `.items` or (`.keys` and `.__getitem__`) and one of it's
    element's length is not `2`.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.weak_value_dictionary.WeakValueDictionary.values
documentation:
Returns value iterator for the weak value dictionary.

Returns
-------
value_iterator : ``_WeakValueDictionaryValueIterator``

Weak value dictionary, which stores it's values weakly referenced.

# Attributes

\_pending_removals

: Pending removals of the weak value dictionary if applicable.

\_iterating

: Whether the weak value dictionary is iterating and how much times.

\_callback

: Callback added to the `WeakValueDictionary`'s weak values.

# Class Attributes

MAX_REPR_ELEMENT_LIMIT

: The maximal amount of items to render by `.__repr__`.

# Notes

`WeakValueDictionary`-s are weakreferable.

Represents a callable's parameter.

Attributes
----------
annotation : `object`
    The parameter's annotation if applicable. Defaults to `None`.
default : `object`
    The default value of the parameter if applicable. Defaults to `None`.
has_annotation : `bool`
    Whether the parameter has annotation.
has_default : `bool`
    Whether the parameter has default value.
name : `str`
    The parameter's name.
positionality : `int`
    Whether the parameter is positional, keyword or such.
    
    Can be set one of the following:
    +----------------------------------------+-----------+
    | Respective Name                        | Value     |
    +========================================+===========+
    | PARAMETER_TYPE_POSITIONAL_ONLY         | 1         |
    +----------------------------------------+-----------+
    | PARAMETER_TYPE_POSITIONAL_AND_KEYWORD  | 2         |
    +----------------------------------------+-----------+
    | PARAMETER_TYPE_KEYWORD_ONLY            | 3         |
    +----------------------------------------+-----------+
    | PARAMETER_TYPE_ARGS                    | 4         |
    +----------------------------------------+-----------+
    | PARAMETER_TYPE_KWARGS                  | 5         |
    +----------------------------------------+-----------+
reserved : `bool`
    Whether the parameter is reserved.
    
    For example at the case of methods, the first parameter is reserved for the `self` parameter.

Analyzer for callable-s.

Can analyze functions, methods, callable objects and types or such.

# Attributes

args_parameter

: If the analyzed callable has `*args` parameter, then this attribute is set to it. Defaults to `None`.

parameters

: The analyzed callable's parameters.

callable

: The analyzed object.

instance_to_async

: Whether the analyzed object can be instanced to async.

  | Respective Name          | Value | Description                                                                    |
  | ------------------------ | ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
  | INSTANCE_TO_ASYNC_FALSE  | 1     | Whether the object is async.                                                   |
  | INSTANCE_TO_ASYNC_TRUE   | 2     | Whether the object is on async callable, but after instancing it, returns one. |
  | INSTANCE_TO_ASYNC_CANNOT | 3     | Whether the object is not async.                                               |

kwargs_parameter

: If the analyzed callable has `**kwargs`, then this attribute is set to it. Defaults to `None`.

method_allocation

: How much parameter is allocated if the analyzed callable is method if applicable.

real_function

: The function wrapped by the given callable.

documentation:
Gets the value of the weak key dictionary which matches the given key.

Parameters
----------
key : `object`
    A key to match.
default : `object` = `None`, Optional
    Default value to return if the given `key` could not be matched.

Returns
-------
value : `object`, `default`
    The key's matched value. If no value was matched returns the `default` value.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.weak_key_dictionary.WeakKeyDictionary.pop
documentation:
Pops the value of the weak key dictionary which matches the given key.

Parameters
----------
key : `object`
    A key to match.
default : `object`, Optional
    Default value to return if the given `key` could not be matched.

Returns
-------
value : `object`, `default`
    The key's matched value. If no value was matched and `default` value is given, then returns that.

Raises
------
KeyError
    If `key` could not be matched and `default` value is was not given either.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.weak_key_dictionary.WeakKeyDictionary.setdefault
documentation:
Returns the value for the given `key`.

If the `key` is not present in the weak key dictionary, then set's the given `default` value as it.

Parameters
----------
key : `object`
    The key to match.
default : `object` = `None`, Optional
    Default value to set and return if `key` is not present in the weak key dictionary.

Returns
-------
value : `default`, `object`
    The matched value, or `default` if none.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.weak_key_dictionary.WeakKeyDictionary.update
documentation:
Updates the weak key dictionary with the given iterable's elements.

Parameters
----------
iterable : `iterable`
    Iterable to extend the weak key dictionary with.
    
    Can be given as an object, which:
    - supports `.items` iterator.
    - supports `.keys` and `.__getitem__`.
    - is `iterable` and each iteration returns a sequence with 2 elements.

Raises
------
TypeError
    The given `iterable` is not `iterable`.
ValueError
    The the given `iterable` sot supports neither `.items` or (`.keys` and `.__getitem__`) and one of it's
    element's length is not `2`.

methods.name: scarletio.utils.weak_key_dictionary.WeakKeyDictionary.values
documentation:
Returns value iterator for the weak key dictionary.

Returns
-------
value_iterator : ``_WeakKeyDictionaryValueIterator``

Weak key dictionary, which stores it's keys weakly referenced.

# Attributes

\_pending_removals

: Pending removals of the weak key dictionary if applicable.

\_iterating

: Whether the weak key dictionary is iterating and how much times.

\_callback

: Callback added to the `WeakKeyDictionary`'s weak keys.

# Class Attributes

MAX_REPR_ELEMENT_LIMIT

: The maximal amount of items to render by `.__repr__`.

# Notes

`WeakKeyDictionary`-s are weakreferable.
